items.why: Classic false friend — 'constipado' looks like 'constipated' but means having a cold; 'estreñido' is constipated.

items.q: How do you say 'My feet hurt'?
items.why: Students treat doler like a normal verb, missing that it works like gustar and agrees with the thing that hurts (los pies, plural).
Students overuse jugar for every sport, but jugar is for games you play; activities like swimming take hacer.

items.q: How do you say 'I play football'?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Juego al fútbol.. “Jugo el fútbol.” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.
It's Junk food. “A packed lunch” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: Which phrase means 'You must drink lots of water'?
items.why: It's Hay que beber mucha agua.. “Bebía mucha agua.” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: 'Juego al tenis desde hace dos años.' For how many years has this person been playing tennis?
items.why: The real figure is 2 years. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
